extra.reasoning: The property requires that in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false. I analyzed how each variable changes. The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (on send_attempt, guarded by NOT opted_out). The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is t1 (optout), which simultaneously sets sent:=false. No transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so once opted_out becomes true it stays true permanently. While opted_out is true: t2 is disabled (its guard requires not opted_out), t39 handles send_attempt by setting sent:=false, and every key_* transition sets sent:=false. Thus there is no way to set sent:=true while opted_out is true, and the entry into opted_out=true (via t1) clears sent to false. Therefore every reachable state with opted_out=true has sent=false, and the temporal property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all executions (the stage variable and its 36 transitions are irrelevant to this property since they only ever set sent:=false). The property holds.
